title: Remove ReadHeaderTimeout
body:
Effectively sets timeout to infinity.
This is needed in order to make `podman` work with `pack`.
The `pack` CLI is keeping one connection for prolonged time.
Closing the connection breaks `pack`'s functionality.

comments.body: There is a tool for which the timeout posses a problem.

The tool is `pack` for building buildpacks.
I am already tying to address it here https://github.com/buildpacks/lifecycle/pull/684.

Please repush with and explanation of why you want this change,  We need @jwhonce to describe why he added it in the first place.  I guess this would allow someone to connect to the service and just hang it?  Is my assumption correct?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *matejvasek*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
@mheon @jwhonce I would love to get this into 3.3.
@rhatdan Correct, without the timeout, we as open to a DOS. The attacker would just open n sockets and never close them until the service hits the configured rlimit.
@jwhonce but even with 20 second limit the attacker still could keep opening a lot of connection right? also how often are user exposing the socket to outer internet?
Am in for reverting this as soon as this is resolved https://github.com/buildpacks/lifecycle/issues/686
